title: Running Tests
description: Learn different ways to execute Playwright tests and configure test runs

questions.question: What is the basic command to run all Playwright tests?
questions.explanation: The command 'npx playwright test' runs all tests in your Playwright test suite.

questions.question: Complete the command to run tests in a specific file:
questions.explanation: You can specify a file path after 'npx playwright test' to run only that file.

questions.question: By default, Playwright runs tests in headless mode.
questions.explanation: Playwright runs tests in headless mode by default for faster execution without opening browser windows.

questions.question: Which flag runs tests in headed (visible browser) mode?
questions.explanation: The --headed flag runs tests with visible browser windows for debugging and observation.

questions.question: Complete the command to run tests in debug mode:
questions.explanation: The --debug flag opens Playwright Inspector for step-by-step test debugging.

questions.question: How do you run tests only in a specific browser?
questions.explanation: Use --project=chromium (or firefox/webkit) to run tests in a specific browser configuration.

questions.question: The --ui flag launches an interactive UI to run and debug tests.
questions.explanation: The --ui flag opens Playwright's UI mode for interactive test execution and debugging.

questions.question: What's wrong with this command?
questions.explanation: The correct command is 'npx playwright test', not 'run'.

questions.question: Which flag generates an HTML report after test execution?
options: HTML reports are generated by default
questions.explanation: Playwright generates HTML reports by default. Use 'npx playwright show-report' to view them.

questions.question: Complete the command to run tests matching a title pattern:
questions.explanation: Use --grep or -g flag to run only tests matching a specific pattern in their title.
